narrative: Your loan application was carefully reviewed by our credit model, which looks at various aspects of your financial profile to assess risk, drawing from patterns in historical bank data from 1970s Germany. The model estimated a 51.5% probability that you might face challenges repaying a loan, which crossed our threshold for denial to protect both you and the bank from potential issues. At the heart of this decision were a few key factors from your situation that stood out as the strongest influences. First, your checking account status, showing a balance below 0 DM, was the top concern—it signaled higher risk compared to most approved applicants, where nearly half had no such account and others had positive balances, pushing the prediction firmly toward caution. Closely following that, your credit history of no prior credits or all paid up raised a red flag; while it might seem positive, it's quite rare among those approved (only about 2% share this), as the model associates it with unproven reliability, further tilting the scales against approval. On a brighter note, your real estate property was a strong positive factor, as it matches a solid asset type held by around 30% of successful applicants and worked to pull the prediction back toward good credit. Your requested amount of 1108 DM is notably lower than the average 3228 DM for approvals, and your short 12-month duration is below the typical 20 months, but these didn't outweigh the main concerns. As a 25-year-old foreign worker with steady 4-7 years of skilled employment, own housing, and no other debts or installment plans, you have many strengths that the model noted favorably, including your single status without dependents adding liability. To move forward, consider building a track record with smaller credits at the bank, improving your checking account balance, or providing more details on savings beyond the <100 DM level—reapplying after these steps could shift the balance in your favor. We're here to help guide you toward a stronger application next time.
